purpose: Determine which public surfaces of one exact source-current PyGRC revision can natively express the accepted L02 discriminator and which are absent or inadequate.
evidence_effect: Capability and missing-surface classification only; no source admission, realization selection, calibration, lane result, or scientific negative.

target_checkout.identity_rule: The audited object is the clean tracked checkout at the exact revision. Every cited source file is additionally bound by repository-relative path and SHA-256 in the retained source-digest inventory.

tracked_file_scope: Tracked Python packaging manifests at repository root that define the pygrc import namespace.
Tracked implementation files under src/pygrc/**, pygrc/**, or any tracked implementation/**/pygrc/** package root selected by the packaging manifests.
Tracked public tests, examples, and documentation that import, export, construct, or exercise an in-scope pygrc surface.
implementation/Phase-8-LGRC9-RestorationIdentityCloseout.json and tracked files it directly identifies as the restoration implementation or contract.
Tracked schema or closeout artifacts directly referenced by an in-scope public surface when needed to determine state identity, restoration coverage, or access scope.
api_scope: Symbols exported from the pygrc package or its public submodules.
Non-underscore methods, properties, constructor parameters, and returned state records on publicly exported classes.
Documented or test-demonstrated public call sequences needed to construct, observe, intervene on, snapshot, restore, or digest a candidate carrier.
Public model-owned transition surfaces for topology, support, capacity, flux, causal pulses, packets, event history, eligibility, and restoration when relevant to a frozen capability question.
evidence_exclusions: Untracked graph-repository files and ambient editor or cache state.
Underscore-prefixed implementation details unless required only to explain why no public surface exposes an observed internal distinction.
Direct mutation of model internals, monkey-patching, copied PyGRC implementation, or private-state injection as evidence of native adequacy.
RCAE candidate outcomes, calibration results, or later realization preferences.
Network sources or a different graph revision.
scope.public_surface_rule: A surface counts as public only when it is exported, non-private on an exported object, or demonstrated as supported by tracked public documentation/tests. Internal state may establish that a distinction exists but cannot by itself establish native public adequacy.
scope.native_rule: A capability is native when PyGRC owns the causally relevant carrier state or transition and exposes the required observation/intervention through the public surface. RCAE construction and orchestration may invoke a native operation but may not compute the claimed combined state or later response and relabel it native.
scope.audit_scope_change_rule: Any scope correction before I01 closure requires a retained P2-I2 change-ledger entry that states the trigger, exact revision, affected questions, and rerun scope. No unrecorded widening is allowed.

scope_corrections.trigger: The in-scope root pyproject.toml declares package-dir = {"" = "src"} and package discovery under src, so the public checkout package is src/pygrc/**.
scope_corrections.correction: Add tracked src/pygrc/** to the implementation-file scope and use ${GRC}/src as the checkout-only PYTHONPATH root.
scope_corrections.rerun_scope: None: the correction was recorded after reading only the already in-scope packaging manifest and before reading any src/pygrc source file or classifying any capability.
scope_corrections.preserved_boundary: Exact graph revision, read-only boundary, public-surface rule, classification contract, output contract, and no-evidence effect remain unchanged.

definitions.adequate: A public native surface satisfies the capability question and preserves the accepted L02 causal boundary without an RCAE-owned substitute for the relevant state or transition.
definitions.inadequate: A public native surface exists and is relevant, but a named missing distinction, intervention, identity, or causal boundary prevents it from satisfying the capability question.
definitions.absent: No qualifying public native surface was found within the frozen tracked-file and API scope at the exact revision.
definitions.unresolved: The frozen evidence cannot distinguish adequacy, inadequacy, or absence without work outside the audit boundary.

classification_contract.producer_rule: For every promising inadequate or absent capability, describe the smallest explicit RCAE producer, constructed transition, or ecology-owned state that could expose the missing relation. This is a demand description, not realization selection or authorization.
classification_contract.fail_closed_rule: Audit failure, absence, inadequacy, or unresolved status cannot become a negative L02 result.

capability_questions.question: Is there a public native non-private carrier with reconstructible identity and declared access scope?
Can at least two attributable contribution paths alter one carrier without source-private response reads?
Is there a carrier-scoped read, susceptibility, or eligibility path that does not require contributor addressing?
Can encounter state, active history, or both persist and be intervened on independently of audit metadata?
Can the audit-only label permutation and common-carrier intervention be expressed without causal bypass?
Can pool write freeze and mode-relevant clamp interventions be expressed?
Can a private-partition counterfactual preserve marginal contributions and opportunity without recreating a common state?
Are reserve, accumulation, mixing, depletion, saturation, leakage, and maintenance observable or classifiably inapplicable?
What native state is covered by restoration identity, and what ecology pool/intervention state would remain external?
Does each candidate surface classify as adequate, inadequate, absent, or unresolved, with a precise reason and evidence reference?
What is the smallest producer or constructed transition needed for each otherwise promising inadequate surface?

allowed_commands.notation: ${GRC} denotes the target checkout; generated scratch output may exist only under /tmp.
commands: git -C ${GRC} ls-files with frozen in-scope pathspecs
rg --files over frozen in-scope tracked directories
rg -n with capability terms over frozen in-scope files
sed -n over frozen in-scope text files
sha256sum over cited frozen in-scope tracked files
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=${GRC}/src python -B -c <read-only import, introspection, or in-memory conformance probe>
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=${GRC}/src python -B -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider <targeted existing test or /tmp-only probe>
conformance_constraints: A conformance probe must import from the exact checkout and record module __file__.
A probe may call public constructors and operations in memory but may not patch, inject private state, write to the graph checkout, or act as candidate execution.
Any generated files, test temporaries, or caches must be directed to /tmp.
Graph HEAD and clean worktree status must be rechecked after audit activity.
prohibited_actions: Any graph-repository file write, formatter, generator, build output, package installation, or dependency update.
Git checkout, switch, reset, clean, commit, stash, merge, rebase, or other graph-repository mutation.
Network access or inspection of a different revision as substitute evidence.
Candidate execution, calibration, source admission, or realization selection.

package_identity_policy.reason: I01 audits the exact checkout rather than an ambient installation. Any dynamic inspection must force the checkout package root through PYTHONPATH, disable bytecode writes, and record the imported module path.
package_identity_policy.separation_rule: If checkout import proves impossible and an installed package becomes necessary, stop and record an audit-scope correction before inspecting it; never merge installed-package facts with checkout facts.

exit_requirements: Frozen audit scope retained before source inspection.
All capability questions resolved into a complete compact matrix.
Exact revision, cited source paths and digests, and command provenance retained.
Public API facts separated from inferred adequacy, missing surfaces, and open questions.
Bounded native shortlist or classified absence retained without source admission or realization selection.
Graph revision unchanged and worktree clean after the audit.
